mcqs.question: Which of the following statements is TRUE about Pushdown Automata (PDA)?

options.text: A PDA can store only a finite amount of information in its stack
A PDA can recognize context-sensitive languages
A PDA must have at least two stack symbols
A PDA can be converted to an equivalent CFG always

mcqs.explanation: Every PDA (even non-deterministic) can be converted to an equivalent CFG using the construction based on PDA transitions, proving decidability of emptiness for CFLs.

mcqs.question: Consider the CFG: S → aSb | ε. Which of the following PDAs accepts the language generated by this grammar?

options.text: PDA with start symbol S and transition (q, ε, S) → (q, aSb)
PDA with start symbol S and transition (q, ε, S) → (q, aS) and (q, ε, S) → (q, b)
PDA with start symbol Z (bottom of stack) and transition (q, ε, Z) → (q, SZ)
PDA with start symbol S and transition (q, ε, S) → (q, a) and (q, ε, S) → (q, b)

mcqs.explanation: The standard conversion from CFG to PDA uses the left-recursive form where S → aSb pushes 'aSb' on the stack, and S → ε pops 'b' while matching input 'a'.

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between a Deterministic PDA (DPDA) and a Non-Deterministic PDA (NPDA)?

options.text: DPDA cannot accept context-free languages that are inherently ambiguous
NPDA always has more stack symbols than DPDA
DPDA can accept all regular languages but NPDA cannot
The difference lies only in the number of states, not in acceptance power

mcqs.explanation: DPDAs accept exactly the deterministic context-free languages (DCFL), which are a proper subset of CFLs; inherently ambiguous CFLs cannot be accepted by any DPDA.

mcqs.question: Given a PDA that accepts by empty stack, which of the following modifications is required to make it accept by final state instead?

options.text: Add a new final state and transition from all states on empty stack to this state
Remove all transitions that pop the stack symbol Z0 (bottom marker)
Add a transition from each state to a final state on any input symbol with stack unchanged
Convert all accepting configurations to final states and keep the stack empty condition

mcqs.explanation: To convert an empty-stack accepting PDA to final-state acceptance, add a new final state qf and for every state q, add transition (q, ε, Z0) → (qf, Z0) to reach the final state when stack becomes empty.

mcqs.question: Which of these languages CANNOT be accepted by any Pushdown Automata?

options.text: L = {ww^R | w ∈ {a,b}*}
L = {a^n b^n c^n | n ≥ 1}
L = {a^i b^j | i ≥ j}
L = {ww | w ∈ {a,b}*}

mcqs.explanation: L = {a^n b^n c^n | n ≥ 1} is not context-free because it requires comparing three counters simultaneously, which exceeds the single-stack capacity of a PDA (pumping lemma for CFLs).

mcqs.question: In the construction of a CFG from a PDA, what do the variables of the grammar represent?

options.text: They represent the stack symbols of the PDA
They represent sequences of states between two pop operations
They represent the input symbols of the PDA
They represent the transition functions of the PDA

mcqs.explanation: In the CFG construction from PDA (using empty stack), each variable A_{pq} represents that the PDA can go from state p to state q while consuming the stack that A_{pq} generates (popping its entire derivation).

mcqs.question: A context-free grammar is ambiguous if:

options.text: It generates an infinite language
There exists a string with more than one leftmost derivation
It contains left-recursive productions
It can be converted to a PDA in multiple ways

mcqs.explanation: A CFG is ambiguous if there exists at least one string in its language that has two distinct parse trees (or equivalently, two different leftmost or rightmost derivations).

mcqs.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the relationship between CFGs and PDAs?

options.text: Every CFG can be converted to a PDA that accepts by final state only, not empty stack
The PDA obtained from a CFG is always deterministic if the grammar is in CNF
The PDA constructed from a CFG uses empty stack acceptance where the start symbol is pushed initially and popped at the end
A PDA can accept a language only if it has a corresponding unambiguous CFG

mcqs.explanation: The standard CFG-to-PDA conversion pushes the start symbol onto the stack initially; the PDA accepts by empty stack when the entire input is consumed and the stack becomes empty (matching S → ε productions).
